title: Rochdale Secures Will Jenkins from South Shields
excerpt: Rochdale has completed the signing of midfielder Will Jenkins from South Shields on a two-year contract. The transfer fee remains undisclosed.
content_html:
Rochdale has announced the acquisition of midfielder Will Jenkins from National League North club South Shields.

Jenkins has signed a two-year deal with the club, although the financial details of the transfer have not been revealed.

The move aims to bolster Rochdale's midfield options ahead of the upcoming season.

title: Historic Rochdale Pub to Be Converted into Flats
excerpt: The Reed Hotel, a 200-year-old pub in Rochdale, is set for transformation into apartments as developers plan a sensitive conversion of the Grade-II listed building.
content_html:
The Reed Hotel, located in Rochdale town centre and built around the late 18th century, will be converted into 10 apartments by Brightwise Properties.

The development plan includes eight one-bedroom and two two-bedroom flats across four floors, with significant interior demolition required.

The building's footprint will largely remain unchanged, with new windows and a proposed single-storey rear extension using matching materials.

Brightwise Properties aims to preserve the historic architectural features of the Grade-II listed building while providing housing in a prime location near the high street.

Planning documents suggest that the conversion will have minimal impact on the building and will benefit the community by increasing housing availability.
review_reason: The article discusses a development project in Rochdale involving a historic building.

title: Rochdale Secures £27k Grant for Chewing Gum Removal
excerpt: Rochdale town centre will benefit from a £27,461 grant aimed at removing chewing gum from its streets. The funding will support a 10-week cleaning programme using specialist equipment.
content_html:
Rochdale has been awarded a grant of £27,461 from the Chewing Gum Task Force to tackle the issue of chewing gum litter in its town centre.

The funding will be used for a 10-week programme that aims to clean approximately 15,700 square metres of pavements, equivalent to two football pitches.

Specialist equipment will be purchased to vapourise the gum, making it easier to remove from the streets.

The initiative was launched due to Rochdale having the most significant gum staining problem in the borough, as identified by local authorities.

Councillor Tricia Ayrton highlighted the importance of responsible gum disposal and warned that littering could result in a Fixed Penalty Notice.
review_reason: The article provides relevant information about a local initiative to clean up Rochdale's streets.
